channel.title: Fabricio Jiménez Morales
channel.description: Recent content on Fabricio Jiménez Morales

item.title: Container Networking by Hand
item.description:
You run a simple docker run command. You get networking. It’s that simple.

But, under the hood, Docker creates network namespaces, virtual Ethernet devices, bridges, routing rules and everything else needed for containers to communicate. I’m following Drew Elliot’s Networking Course lessons in networking; the first lesson is mostly about how networking happens in containers. I followed the course, took notes and revised them, and took the chance to revise a few networking concepts.

item.title: Dev Contaniners and SSH
item.description: Development containers don't need private keys explicitly to communicate with GitHub, and I find that pretty neat.

item.title: Two Linux Security Tips
item.description: Two small habits to improve Linux security

item.title: My First and Best Linux Lesson
item.description: Creating a new user in Linux by hand can teach you more than anything else if you're a beginner
